title: Cyclothon and cultural events held in Howrah to promote voter awareness
author: Jayatri Nag
short_desc: Ahead of assembly elections, the Election Commission of India organized a Cyclothon in Howrah to boost voter awareness, utilizing popular character Chhota Bheem and cultural programs. A special tramcar ride and voter awareness boat ferry are also part of the state-wide campaign, aiming for inclusive
long_desc: Kolkata: With Election Commission of India (ECI) conducting Systematic Voters’ Education and Electoral Participation (SVEEP) Outreach, a Cyclothon was organized in Howrah on Friday morning to create vote related awareness, ahead of upcoming assembly elections. In addition, popular animated character Chhota Bheem was used to boost voter awareness. A special tramcar ride will also be conducted from Esplanade as part of its state-wide Voter Awareness Campaign.The cycle rally was flagged off at 7 AM from the Howrah Bridge Check Post and proceeded via the Rail Museum, before concluding at Ramkrishnapur Ferry Ghat. Cyclothon was followed by several cultural programmes, which emphasized the importance of voting and the festive spirit of elections were conveyed through folk songs, dance, and drama. Various other SVEEP initiatives were also kick-started, including a voter awareness boat ferry, ECI said.In Howrah district, the event commenced with a Cyclothon that witnessed participation from a…

title: Kerala Polls: Tharoor says any CPI(M)-BJP deal won't work, Congress has people's support
author: PTI
short_desc: Congress MP Shashi Tharoor asserts that any CPI(M) and BJP alliance will not succeed in elections. He believes the CPI(M) aims to split anti-incumbency votes by benefiting from BJP's impact on Congress votes. Tharoor emphasises Congress's "deal with the people" and urges voters to support the UDF to
long_desc: Alappuzha: Congress MP Shashi Tharoor on Friday said any alleged deal between the CPI(M) and the BJP would not work in the elections, asserting that the Congress has a  deal with the people .Speaking to reporters during an election campaign in Kayamkulam, Tharoor said if any such understanding exists, it could only be between the CPI(M) and the BJP. The Left's aim is to continue in power. The BJP's aim is to eliminate the Congress. So if there is any deal, it will be between the Left and the BJP,  he said.Also Read: Kerala polls go high-tech as AI reshapes political messagingHe alleged that the Left believes it can benefit if anti-incumbency votes are split. They know that if the BJP eats into Congress votes, it will benefit them. That is the deal,  he said.Tharoor said the Congress, on the other hand, has the deal with the people. We are asking people to stand with us. If we win, you will lead the state. A new government will address Kerala's issues,  he said.He added that voters are…

title: Violence erupts in Basanti as BJP campaign clashes with TMC, police injured
author: Jayatri Nag
short_desc: Major violence erupted in Basanti, South 24 Parganas. Miscreants allegedly attacked BJP workers and police during a campaign event. Several police personnel and BJP workers sustained injuries. Eight miscreants were arrested. A large contingent of CAPF and police forces arrived at the scene.
long_desc: Kolkata: Major violence broke out in South 24 Parganas district's Basanti, with miscreants allegedly beating up BJP workers and attacking police personnel, during the campaign of Basanti BJP candidate Bikash Sardar near Basanti Bazar area. It has been learnt that several police personnel and at least 8-10 BJP workers were injured in the incident. Atleats 8 miscreants were arrested, police sources said. A huge CAPF and a huge police force reached the spot after the incident. A purported video showed that a person was severely beaten up by bamboo sticks by a group of miscreants. The veracity of the video has not been verified by ET. A RAF personnel was also seen running away when the clash broke out. Also Read: Amit Shah to release chargesheet against TMC in West Bengal on March 28Earlier,…

title: Kerala polls: SDPI gives in to pressure, withdraws candidate in Manjeshwar as IUML worried over split in Muslim votes
author: ET Bureau
short_desc: The Social Democratic Party of India (SDPI) withdrew its candidate from Manjeshwar following pressure from local Muslim leaders and the UDF, aiming to prevent a split in minority votes. This move comes as the BJP eyes the coastal constituency, where the IUML narrowly won in 2016. Despite this, a Kon
long_desc: The social democratic party of India (SDPI) on Thursday withdrew its candidate from Manjeshwar after it gave in to sustained pressure from the local Muslim community leaders and the United Democratic Front (UDF), of which Indian Union Muslim League (IUML) is a major constituent.IUML, which has a dominant presence in the constituency bordering Karnataka. The coastal constituency has a strong BJP presence as well, leaving the IUML and its larger partner in the UDF, the Congress, on the edge. The UDF was worried after the SDPI’s stayed firm on a contest as that would lead to a split in the minority votes. Former Kerala BJP president K Surendran is contesting from here.The BJP, which has been expanding its footprint in the constituency since the 1980s. Its vote share has been improving since…
